To a stirred solution of benzyl alcohol (108 mg, 1.00 mmol, 1.0 equiv) in DCM (10 mL) was added Dess-Martin periodinane (636 mg, 1.50 mmol, 1.5 equiv) and NaHCO3 (252 mg, 3.00 mmol, 3.0 equiv) at 0 °C. The reaction was warmed to room temperature and stirred for 1 h. The mixture was diluted with diethyl ether (30 mL) and quenched with saturated Na2S2O3 and saturated NaHCO3 (15 mL each). The layers were separated, and the aqueous layer was extracted with diethyl ether (2 × 15 mL). The combined organic layers were washed with brine (15 mL), dried over Na2SO4, filtered, and concentrated. The crude residue was purified by flash chromatography on silica gel (hexanes/EtOAc, 95:5) to afford benzaldehyde as a colorless liquid (95 mg, 90%).
